2605.23163.title: Fast-dDrive: Efficient Block-Diffusion VLM for Autonomous Driving
2605.23163.paper_summary: End-to-end autonomous driving via Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models demands a precarious balance between high-fidelity trajectory planning and efficient inference. Existing paradigms typically fall short: autoregressive (AR) VLAs are memory-bandwidth-bound on edge hardware and prone to exposure-bias drift, while full-sequence diffusion models preclude KV-cache reuse and suffer from "logical leakage" that violates the fundamental perceive-then-plan causality. We present Fast-dDrive, a block-diffusion VLA that performs bidirectional refinement within semantic units while enforcing strict causal ordering across them. Leveraging the observation that driving VLAs often emit structured JSON-like outputs, Fast-dDrive freezes structural tokens into a section scaffold and employs a section-aware training recipe that prioritizes safety-critical planning. We further introduce Scaffold Speculative Decoding to achieve AR-equivalent quality at significantly higher throughput. Finally, we propose a low-overhead test-time scaling scheme: by forking $N$ stochastic trajectory rollouts from a single shared-prefix KV cache and averaging them, we effectively suppress prediction variance at a fractional computational cost. Empirical results demonstrate that Fast-dDrive redefines the speed-accuracy frontier for driving agents. On the WOD-E2E test set, Fast-dDrive achieves SOTA ADE@3s and ADE@5s, alongside the highest RFS among diffusion-based VLAs; on nuScenes, it reduces average L2 error to $0.32$m (a $22\%$ improvement). When integrated with SGLang, our framework delivers $12\times$ throughput speedup over the AR baseline, narrowing the gap between high-capacity VLAs and the efficiency demands of real-time on-vehicle deployment.
2605.23163.paper_summary_zh: 端到端的自動駕駛透過視覺-語言-行動（VLA）模型需要在高保真軌跡規劃和高效推理之間保持微妙的平衡。<lb/>現有的範式通常無法滿足這一需求：自回歸（AR）VLA在邊緣硬體上受到記憶帶寬的限制，並且容易受到曝光偏差漂移的影響，而全序列擴散模型則無法重用KV快取，並且遭受“邏輯洩漏”的問題，這違反了基本的感知-再計劃因果關係。<lb/>我們提出了Fast-dDrive，一種區塊擴散VLA，它在語義單元內執行雙向精煉，同時在它們之間強制嚴格的因果順序。<lb/>利用駕駛VLA經常產生結構化JSON類輸出的觀察，Fast-dDrive將結構性標記凍結在一個區段支架中，並採用一種區段感知的訓練方案，優先考慮安全關鍵的規劃。<lb/>我們進一步引入了支架推測解碼，以在顯著更高的吞吐量下實現AR等效的質量。<lb/>最後，我們提出了一種低開銷的測試時間擴展方案：通過從單一共享前綴KV快取中分叉$N$個隨機軌跡回滾並對其進行平均，我們有效地抑制了預測方差，並以較小的計算成本達成。<lb/>實證結果顯示，Fast-dDrive重新定義了駕駛代理的速度-準確性邊界。在WOD-E2E測試集上，Fast-dDrive實現了SOTA ADE@3s和ADE@5s，以及擴散基VLA中最高的RFS；在nuScenes上，它將平均L2誤差降低到$0.32$m（改善了$22\%$）。<lb/>當與SGLang集成時，我們的框架在AR基準上提供了$12\times$的吞吐量加速，縮小了高容量VLA與實時車載部署的效率需求之間的差距。
2605.23163.author: Kewei Zhang et.al.
2605.23163.authors: Kewei Zhang, Jin Wang, Sensen Gao, Chengyue Wu, Yulong Cao, Songyang Han, Boris Ivanovic, Langechuan Liu, Marco Pavone, Song Han, Daquan Zhou, Enze Xie
